---
title: "Vacations"
description: |
  Where and when?
date: '2026-01-20'
image: "vacation.png"
categories:
    - Adventure
    - Regional
format: html
toc: true
lightbox: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  error: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
library(here)
source(here("packages.R"))
source(here("functions.R"))

series_filter = 6

polls = pollresults(6) %>%
  group_by(question) %>%
  mutate(N = sum(votes)) %>%
  ungroup()

# function to summarize a two-option question
summarize_two_option <- function(df) {
  stopifnot(nrow(df) == 2)
  N <- unique(df$N)
  lead <- df |> arrange(desc(votes)) |> slice(1)
  trail <- df |> arrange(desc(votes)) |> slice(2)

  bt <- binom.test(x = lead$votes, n = N, p = 0.5)  # exact test vs 50/50

  tibble(
    question      = df$question[1],
    N             = N,
    leader        = lead$option,
    leader_votes  = lead$votes,
    leader_share  = lead$share,
    trailer       = trail$option,
    trailer_votes = trail$votes,
    trailer_share = trail$share,
    margin_votes  = lead$votes - trail$votes,
    margin_share  = lead$share - trail$share,
    p_value_50_50 = bt$p.value,
    ci_lower      = bt$conf.int[1],
    ci_upper      = bt$conf.int[2],
    flips_to_flip = floor((lead$votes - trail$votes)/2) + 1L
  )
}

by_q = polls %>%
  group_by(question) %>%
  group_modify(~ summarize_two_option(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()
```

![](vacation.png)

# This week, we take a tour of our peers vacation preferences. 

## Vote counts and shares

```{r}
#| label: fig-bars
#| fig-cap: "Votes and shares for each question (faceted)."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
pal <- c(
  "Hot" = "#F5871B", "Cold" = "#76AAB7",
  "Nature" = "#59A14F", "Urban" = "#777f8f",
  "Relaxation" = "#bd595a", "Exploration & Adventure" = "#edbe48",
  "Domestic" = "#B07AA1", "International" = "#4E79A7"
)

polls %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(option, votes), y = votes, fill = option)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(votes, " (", percent(share, accuracy = 0.1), ")")),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3.8) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Votes") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  facet_wrap(~ question, scales = "free_x") +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## How clear are the leads?

Is the lead clearly more than 50/50, and if so, by how much?

```{r}
#| label: summary-table
by_q %>%
  transmute(
    question,
    N,
    `Leader (votes)` = sprintf("%s (%d)", leader, leader_votes),
    `Trailer (votes)`= sprintf("%s (%d)", trailer, trailer_votes),
    `Leader share`   = percent(leader_share, 0.1),
    `Margin (votes)` = margin_votes,
    `Margin (points)`= percent(margin_share, 0.1),
    `p vs 50/50`     = signif(p_value_50_50, 3),
    `Leader 95% CI`  = sprintf("[%.2f, %.2f]", ci_lower, ci_upper),
    `Votes to flip winner` = flips_to_flip
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-leader-ci
#| fig-cap: "Leader shares with 95% confidence intervals."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.8
by_q %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_rev(question), x = leader_share, color = question)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_lower, xmax = ci_upper), height = 0.15) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0.4, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Leader's share of votes", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
#| label: connection-table
#| fig-cap: "Overlap bounds and independence expectations for selected pairs."
# Prepare a helper to compute min/max feasible overlap and independence expectation
bounds_pair <- function(a, b, N) {
  min_ov <- max(0, a + b - N)
  max_ov <- min(a, b)
  exp_ov <- N * (a / N) * (b / N)  # independence expectation
  tibble(min_overlap = min_ov, expected_overlap = exp_ov, max_overlap = max_ov)
}

# Extract counts
N <- unique(polls$N); stopifnot(length(N) == 1)  # here N = 33
count <- polls |>
  select(question, option, votes) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = c(question, option), values_from = votes)

pairs <- tribble(
  ~pair,                               ~a, ~b,
  "Hot & Nature",                       count$Climate_Hot,       count$Setting_Nature,
  "Hot & Domestic",                     count$Climate_Hot,       count$Destination_Domestic,
  "Nature & Domestic",                  count$Setting_Nature,    count$Destination_Domestic,
  "Adventure & Hot",                    count$`Style_Exploration & Adventure`, count$Climate_Hot,
  "Adventure & Nature",                 count$`Style_Exploration & Adventure`, count$Setting_Nature,
  "Adventure & Domestic",               count$`Style_Exploration & Adventure`, count$Destination_Domestic
) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(tmp = list(bounds_pair(a, b, N))) |>
  unnest(tmp) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    min_overlap = as.integer(min_overlap),
    max_overlap = as.integer(max_overlap)
  )

pairs %>%
  transmute(
    pair,
    `Min overlap` = min_overlap,
    `Expected if independent` = sprintf("%.1f", expected_overlap),
    `Max overlap` = max_overlap
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kable()
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-waffles
#| fig-cap: "Waffle charts (3×11 grid per question)."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 7
make_waffle <- function(df, rows = 3, cols = 11) {
  stopifnot(nrow(df) == 2)
  N <- unique(df$N)
  tibble(
    id = 1:N,
    option = c(rep(df$option[1], df$votes[1]),
               rep(df$option[2], df$votes[2])),
    question = df$question[1]
  ) |>
    mutate(
      row = (id - 1) %/% cols + 1,
      col = (id - 1) %% cols + 1
    )
}

waffles <- polls |>
  group_by(question) |>
  group_modify(~ make_waffle(.x)) |>
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(option = factor(option, levels = c("Hot","Cold",
                                            "Domestic","International",
                                            "Nature","Urban",
                                            "Relaxation","Exploration & Adventure")))

ggplot(waffles, aes(x = col, y = 3 - row + 1, fill = option)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4, width = 0.95, height = 0.95) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  facet_wrap(~ question)
```
```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: true
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(knitr)

# Poll results -------------------------------------------------------------
results <- tribble(
  ~question,     ~option,                       ~votes,
  "Climate",     "Cold",                        9,
  "Climate",     "Hot",                        24,
  "Destination", "International",               9,
  "Destination", "Domestic",                   24,
  "Setting",     "Urban",                       9,
  "Setting",     "Nature",                     24,
  "Style",       "Relaxation",                 10,
  "Style",       "Exploration & Adventure",    23
)

# Summaries for convenience ------------------------------------------------
majorities <- results |>
  group_by(question) |>
  mutate(n = sum(votes)) |>
  slice_max(votes, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  transmute(
    question,
    fav = option,
    votes = votes,
    n = n,
    prop = votes / n,
    margin = votes - (n - votes)
  )

# Exact binomial tests vs 50/50, and effect size (Cohen's h)
stats <- majorities |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    bt = list(binom.test(votes, n, p = 0.5, alternative = "two.sided")),
    p_value  = bt$p.value,
    conf_low = bt$conf.int[1],
    conf_high= bt$conf.int[2],
    cohen_h  = abs(2*asin(sqrt(prop)) - 2*asin(sqrt(0.5)))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(-bt)

# A helper to pull values inline
pull_stat <- function(q, col) {
  stats |>
    filter(question == q) |>
    pull({{ col }})
}

pull_majority <- function(q, col) {
  majorities |>
    filter(question == q) |>
    pull({{ col }})
}
```

# TL;DR

Climate: `r pull_majority("Climate", fav)` wins with `r percent(pull_majority("Climate", prop), accuracy = 1)` (`r pull_majority("Climate", votes)` of `r pull_majority("Climate", n)` votes; margin `r pull_majority("Climate", margin)`).

Destination: `r pull_majority("Destination", fav)` wins with `r percent(pull_majority("Destination", prop), accuracy = 1)` (`r pull_majority("Destination", votes)` of `r pull_majority("Destination", n)`).

Setting: `r pull_majority("Setting", fav)` wins with `r percent(pull_majority("Setting", prop), accuracy = 1)` (`r pull_majority("Setting", votes)` of `r pull_majority("Setting", n)`).

Style: `r pull_majority("Style", fav)` wins with `r percent(pull_majority("Style", prop), accuracy = 1)` (`r pull_majority("Style", votes)` of `r pull_majority("Style", n)`).

Fun finding: the first three questions all landed on the exact same split (24 vs 9) so roughly 73% vs 27% across climate, destination, and setting. That’s impressively consistent!

# Visualizing the results

```{r}
#| label: fig-share
#| fig-cap: "Share of votes by question and option (dashed line marks 50/50)."
#| fig-alt: "Stacked bars showing the share of votes per question for each option. A dashed line at 50% shows the midpoint."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(results, aes(x = fct_rev(question), y = votes, fill = option)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill", width = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = 2, color = "gray50") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = NULL) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Share of votes", title = "Vacation poll preferences by question (share of votes)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
